learnings.description: H4 headings with 'Check N:' prefix caused substring collisions in validator marker matching. Validator markers must use semantic heading levels, not numeric prefixes.
learnings.resolution: Promoted drift check headings from H4 to H3 and removed numeric prefix
learnings.preventionRule: Always use semantic heading levels for validator markers — avoid numeric prefixes that create substring collision risk

learnings.description: Config flags without deterministic workflow instructions are dead features — agents skip non-mandatory steps. Behavioral config values (like taskTracking) must have explicit Workflow Impact annotations.
learnings.resolution: Added Workflow Impact annotations to all behavioral config values in config-handling.md. Converted 'Tips' to enforcement gates with 'protocol breach' language.
learnings.preventionRule: Every config flag that alters agent behavior must have a corresponding Workflow Impact annotation specifying exactly which phase/step it affects and what the agent must do differently

learnings.description: Adding a file to core/templates/ causes generator to auto-load it as a spec-type template. Audit artifact templates must be defined inline in their core module, not in core/templates/.
learnings.resolution: Defined dependency-audit.md template inline in core/dependency-safety.md instead of as a separate file
learnings.preventionRule: Do not add non-spec-template files to core/templates/ — the generator auto-loads ALL .md files there as spec-type templates
reconsiderWhen: generator/generate.py is refactored to support template categories

learnings.description: When adding new *_MARKERS constants to validate.py, they must be added to BOTH the per-platform validate_platform() function AND the cross-platform consistency check loop. Missing one location causes silent validation gaps.
learnings.resolution: Established Gap 31 enforcement rule: markers in both locations in same commit
learnings.preventionRule: When adding any new *_MARKERS constant to validate.py: add to BOTH validate_platform() AND the cross-platform consistency check loop in the same commit
reconsiderWhen: validate.py is refactored to auto-discover marker constants

learnings.description: Claude Code hooks schema requires nested 'hooks' array with typed entries (type: command), not a flat matcher + command property. The settings.json schema validation rejects flat hook structures.
learnings.resolution: Used nested hooks schema: matcher + hooks array of typed hook objects
learnings.preventionRule: When writing Claude Code hooks, always use the nested schema: { matcher: {...}, hooks: [{ type: 'command', command: '...' }] }

learnings.description: Cross-platform marker sync between validate.py and test_platform_consistency.py drifts silently. The Gap 31 rule (L-context-aware-dispatch-1) covers validate.py internal consistency but not the test file. 7 entire marker categories were missing from tests, and 2 from validate.py, despite both files being modified in the same changeset.
learnings.resolution: Added all missing categories to test_platform_consistency.py (coherence, delegation, engineering_discipline, dependency_safety, interview, task_tracking, regression) and to validate.py (simplicity, data_handling). Aligned from_plan and writing_quality markers.
learnings.preventionRule: Extend Gap 31 rule to three-way sync: when adding *_MARKERS to validate.py, add to (1) validate_platform(), (2) cross-platform consistency loop, AND (3) test_platform_consistency.py REQUIRED_MARKERS in the same commit

learnings.description: Completed specs can pass all existing checks while missing the Documentation Review section in implementation.md. The lint-spec-artifacts.py check exists but was added after earlier specs were completed, creating a gap for newly completed specs that skip Phase 4 docs review.
learnings.resolution: Added Documentation Review section to production-learnings/implementation.md. The lint-spec-artifacts.py check now catches this for all completed specs.
learnings.preventionRule: Phase 4 completion must include Documentation Review section in implementation.md before marking spec as completed. The lint-spec-artifacts.py check enforces this retroactively.

learnings.description: CLAUDE.md mode count (15 vs 16) and key modules list fell out of sync when the learn mode was added. These are manually maintained values that drift when new modes are added.
learnings.resolution: Updated CLAUDE.md to say 16 modes and added core/learnings.md and core/dispatcher.md to key modules list.
learnings.preventionRule: When adding a new mode to core/mode-manifest.json, update CLAUDE.md mode count and mode list in the same commit
reconsiderWhen: CLAUDE.md mode count is auto-generated from mode-manifest.json

learnings.description: Adversarial evaluation sub-agents score uniformly high (8/10) across all dimensions because they are the same LLM confirming its own work. Both plan-mode-blocking-enforcement and dependency-introduction-gate scored exactly 8/10 on all 4 dimensions despite acknowledged gaps (no unit tests for gate logic, no black-box testing of hook behavior, no registry API exercise). The evaluator notes gaps but rationalizes them instead of scoring lower. Fresh-context dispatch is insufficient to overcome structural self-confirmation bias.
learnings.preventionRule: Do not rely on same-LLM adversarial evaluation as a quality gate. Uniform high scores (e.g., 8/10 across all dimensions) are a signal of self-confirmation bias, not actual quality. Evaluation scores from same-model sub-agents should be treated as advisory, not authoritative. Consider external validation, concrete test coverage metrics, or human review for actual quality assurance.
reconsiderWhen: core/evaluation.md is rearchitected to use a different model or external evaluator for adversarial scoring
